data.title: Why Coinbase is so interested in acquiring Echo for $375 million
data.description:
Deng Tong, Jinse Finance

On October 21, 2025, Coinbase acquired Echo, a leading on-chain financing platform, for approximately $375 million. The Echo platform makes it easier for community members to raise funds and invest, whether through Echo's private offering or Sonar's public offering.

What is Echo? Who are its founders? Why is Coinbase so interested in it? Will the ICO boom return?

I. What is Echo? Who are its founders? Founded in 2023 by crypto OG Cobie (Jordan Fish), former fund manager Lexie, and others, Echo is an on-chain fundraising platform that allows blockchain projects to conduct private or public token issuances to the community or investors through platform tools. The platform's mission is to help "new startups or token projects join private financing rounds alongside the professional investment community." Its focus is on "enabling projects to raise funds directly from the community," "enabling earlier investor participation," and "making the process more transparent." In May 2025, Echo launched its Sonar product, tweeting on X: "We're making on-chain fundraising accessible to everyone. Introducing Sonar." Its founder, Cobie, is a prominent figure in the crypto space. Cobie entered the crypto space in 2013, initially as a speculator and hobbyist, investing in assets such as BTC, ETH, and DOGE in the early stages. In 2020, he founded the crypto podcast "UpOnly," co-hosting the show with Chris Dixon. Cobie has hosted guests like Elon Musk, Vitalik Buterin, CZ, and SBF, making it one of the industry's most popular podcasts. Cobie is also a top investor. During the bull market, he participated in the angel rounds of numerous early-stage projects, including Lido, Arbitrum, ENS, dYdX, and Friend.tech. This also laid the foundation for his subsequent creation of the on-chain fundraising platform, Echo. In response to Coinbase's recent acquisition of Cobie, Cobie stated at X: "Echo will remain a standalone platform under its existing brand, but we will integrate Sonar's public sale product into Coinbase, potentially introducing new ways for founders to reach investors and providing investors with access to Coinbase." Coinbase stated that it plans to expand Echo's infrastructure beyond cryptocurrencies, ultimately supporting tokenized securities and real-world assets. This acquisition complements Coinbase's previous acquisition of token management platform LiquiFi. Coinbase also invested $25 million to relaunch Cobie's UpOnly podcast.

II. Echo's Star Products

In early 2024, Echo launched Private Group Investing, supporting private group fundraising, on-chain USDC settlement, and a multi-round distribution mechanism. Mid-year, it launched the Investor Dashboard, providing investors with investment history, project tracking, and return display functions. By the end of the year, it launched the Founder Portal, a fundraising backend system for project parties, supporting token allocation, whitelist control, and smart contract deployment. In May 2025, it launched Sonar, a self-hosted public offering tool. Private Group Investing and Sonar are two of Echo's star products. Private Group Investing allows projects to launch private sales through a "group leader + community member" structure, while Echo provides on-chain tools, smart contracts, USDC circulation, and standard processes. Sonar allows projects to conduct "self-hosted" public token sales. This means that developers can use the Sonar tool to raise funds directly from the community, rather than being limited to traditional private sales. Echo's most notable success story is Plasma. On May 27, 2025, Plasma officially announced that it would use Echo's new token sale infrastructure, Sonar, for its public sale. Sonar handles KYC verification, jurisdiction screening, and other checks on behalf of Plasma, while Plasma is responsible for sale execution, including the transfer of all funds and tokens. MegaLabs' case has also attracted attention. In December 2024, MegaLabs raised $10 million in less than three minutes using the Echo platform. MegaLabs co-founder Shuyao Kong revealed that the company's initial goal was to raise $4.2 million, which they achieved in just 56 seconds after launching the crowdsale. Due to overwhelming investor demand, the project raised an additional $5.8 million, which was fully funded within 70 seconds. Furthermore, according to Cobie, Ethena, which caused a stir when USDe launched in 2024, was also a key early fundraising project for Echo and one of the first projects Echo helped to close. Third, why is Coinbase so interested in Echo? Coinbase addressed this question in a blog post: Creating a more complete capital market, building a full-stack solution from launch to financing to the secondary market. The "why" is simple. We want to create more accessible, efficient, and transparent capital markets. However, today, founders often struggle to raise capital, and individual investors lack access to private token sales. Echo solves this problem by allowing projects to raise funds directly from the community, either through private offerings or by launching their own public token sales using Sonar. Integrating Echo's tools will help us enable more direct community engagement and connect projects with capital entirely on-chain. While we will initially conduct crypto token sales through Sonar, we plan to leverage Echo's infrastructure to gradually expand support to tokenized securities and real-world assets. Echo has made significant progress in opening up private markets, helping approximately 300 projects raise over $200 million since its launch. Its new, independently operated public token sale product, Sonar, has also seen initial success, powering Plasma's XPL token sale. With this acquisition, we are building a full-stack solution for crypto projects and investors, covering everything from launch to fundraising to secondary trading.

  • For builders: Easier access to capital and community-aligned fundraising tools like Echo for private investment groups and Sonar for self-hosted public token sales;

  • For investors: New, differentiated opportunities previously unavailable through trusted platforms like Echo or directly through Sonar;

  • For the on-chain economy: Creating a more efficient, transparent, and globally accessible capital market to drive innovation and growth.

Echo complements our recent acquisition of Liquifi, which simplifies token creation and cap table management for early-stage teams. Liquifi strengthens our ability to provide early-stage support to entrepreneurs, while Echo extends that support to fundraising. Combined with our existing strengths in exchange listings, custody, staking, trading, and fundraising, we are now able to support token issuers and investors throughout the entire lifecycle, from issuance to fundraising and into the secondary market.

Fourth, will the ICO boom make a comeback?

The significance of Coinbase's acquisition goes far beyond corporate expansion. Driven by Coinbase, a wave of regulated, community-driven token sales may be emerging, which could have a profound impact on future investment models in the cryptocurrency space.

According to Tiger Research, platforms such as Sonar, Buildpad, Legion, and Kaito are leading a new wave of compliant launch platforms, whose KYC and transparency measures combine accessibility with investor protection. In 2017, countless blockchain projects, such as Ethereum, Chainlink, and Filecoin, emerged through initial coin offerings (ICOs). However, the ICO craze quickly cooled due to regulatory concerns. The SEC classified many ICOs as unregistered securities offerings. However, Sonar's ICO model differs from previous offerings—it is conducted through a transparent, self-hosted process. Echo's Sonar product perfectly aligns with this trend: it allows founders to conduct their own public token sales directly on blockchains like Base, Solana, Cardano, and Hyperliquid.

For Coinbase, integrating Sonar transforms its exchange from a trading hub into a global fundraising portal, giving verified users access to early-stage investment opportunities previously only available to venture capital firms.

While consolidating its position as a leading exchange in the industry, Coinbase also offers a new possibility for the crypto-financial market: the "ICO 2.0 era" may be upon us, with its compliance and transparency.

data.title: History is repeating itself: A strong year-end rebound is underway
data.description:
Source: Digital Asset Research, Compiled by Shaw Jinse Finance. Today, I'll explain why this situation is incredibly rare and could trigger the largest volatility in the cryptocurrency market in nearly five years. After nearly a year of stagnation, the market is experiencing unprecedented divisions. In fact, eleven months after Trump's inauguration, cryptocurrency prices have barely budged. In other words, the so-called "crypto government" has had no impact on prices. But that's about to change. It's unusual to see such polarized market sentiment as we approach all-time highs. The reason is timing. Most people are still trapped in four-year cycles, while macroeconomic cycles are clearly visible. We're facing trade wars, physical wars, inflation fears, recession rumors, and interest rate cuts, yet the market remains within striking distance of all-time highs. This is a classic example of a correction before a powerful rally. If you only look at headlines, sentiment, and prices, you'd think this market is crashing. But the timing suggests otherwise. From Bitcoin, stocks, gold, and even memes, signals are converging across asset classes, and the kind of convergence we're seeing now hasn't occurred in over five years. Today, I'll break down why this matters and what it portends for what's coming next. Bitcoin and the Wheel of Cycles Let's start with Bitcoin and its current 90-day cycle. As we know, the 90-day cycle is extremely important in Gann theory because it divides the year into 4 equal parts, forming a complete 360-degree cycle. If we extrapolate 90 days from the low of October 17th, you will find that the market has made highs and lows every 90 degrees, all the way back to the low of October 24th of last year. This cycle indicates that the low on the 17th marked the end of a full cycle that began with the low on October 24, 2024. Now we can look at the weekly cycle to see if the 90-day cycle coincides with the 90-week cycle, and it does. The weekly low following the January 2024 ETF launch occurs exactly 90 weeks after October 17, 2025. Furthermore, this all fits within our 45-degree cycle, which is half of the 90-degree cycle and divides the circle into 8 equal parts. If we look 45 weeks forward and 45 weeks backward from the January 2024 low, we find the 2023 banking crisis low (135 weeks), and the week before the December 2024 weekly high. Both of these are important market turning points.

This cycle has brought us to today's low, which is within these important time factors.

If it were that simple, the market would give us a clear layout and simple price action leading to these lows.

Unfortunately, it is not that simple, and the main reason many people get washed out at these key time factors is that they only focus on price, which appears to be a price collapse.

This is why people call it a whipsaw. In the chart below, starting with the 30, 45, 60, 90, and 135 points, each significant weekly time factor resulted in very poor weekly price action designed to scare off market participants. However, what happened after these poor weekly moves? The market reversed higher. If this isn't enough to convince us that this is a significant low, we can further examine the monthly chart below, where we can see a seven-month low-to-low cycle that has persisted since the FTX crash bottomed. This makes this month a monthly low. The next chart offers a compelling insight into market psychology. Every year in this cycle, January's high plays a crucial role. The market always seems to find a way back to its opening range at the beginning of the year to absorb liquidity. This year is no exception. What's most interesting is that this sector reached extreme optimism at the beginning of the year, only to plunge into complete panic by the end. This effectively became support, with the price level that was once a source of greed now a source of fear. The most similar scenarios, both in terms of charts and price action, are those of 2023 and 2020. Both years were generally stable, but saw significant volatility between the fourth quarter and the second quarter of the following year.

2023 (shown below) was the last time the market traded within the green channel that supported this bull market.

Anyone who traded that year will tell you it was psychologically devastating. The market languished throughout the year and seemed to be getting worse until October, when all hope for a bull market was dashed.

The market rallied almost 200%, breaking through its all-time high, much faster than anyone expected. That's why I believe a similar price shock could occur today. Finally, we return to 2020, the true five-year cycle we've been talking about for months. People were exhausted and ready to give up, yet we saw the same pattern play out in 2020 as we've been closely watching. From January to November, it was flat again. The timing suggests a 30-week gap between the COVID-19-induced plunge and the real start of the rebound. A very similar plunge occurred in April of this year, when the market reset 60 months after the COVID-19 outbreak, and we're only 28 weeks into this rally. This suggests that things are still in line with the repeating rhythm of the five-year cycle. In fact, this 30-week period would take us to the first week of November, when the real rally begins, which is exactly when the big gains of last year and the year before began. Last year, Trump's election on November 4th sent the market soaring. The year before that, ETF news broke in early November, sending the market soaring as well. So, what news are we betting on over the next two weeks that will give the market an opportunity to seize? Now that we’ve seen Bitcoin’s cycles, let’s take a step back and see how the macro picture is leading us towards a 2021-style rally. If we start with this simple cycle chart of the S&P (which we’ve been highlighting all year), you can see that the sideways movement in September/October is just as our cycles predict, and is conducive to more upside in early November. People have completely lost their cool about the Q4 rally now that we're a month in, but they just don't seem to understand that this has been going on for nearly a century. Looking back at the S&P Composite Index chart going back to 1928, we see that year-end rallies typically begin in the last week of October. This is exactly what happened in 2023 and 2024, with both year-end rallies starting in the last week of October. But what was the stock market like in 2020, before the massive cryptocurrency craze? Well, let's take a look at the comparison below. On the left is 2020, and on the right is the same period in 2025. The two are remarkably similar, to say the least. Bitcoin vs. Gold It wasn't just stocks that stole the limelight from cryptocurrencies this year. Gold also garnered attention, with people lining up in the streets to buy it, just like during the 1849 gold rush. Below, we can see how similar the timeframes are compared to the 2020 gold price rally. The period from low to high was exactly 24 months. I'm not analyzing a gold top or making any predictions here; I'm simply pointing out that the timeframes from bottom to top for these moves are identical. If we plug these points into a chart comparing Bitcoin to gold, here’s what it looks like. You can see that from the 2020 highs, gold experienced exactly the same 24-month rally before any major move in Bitcoin. Now, gold is 24 months into that rally, and Bitcoin is at the same point in its cycle about to make a major move. The coup de grace for this macro view of the five-year cycle in full swing is the resurgence of meme stocks and small-cap stocks. If we look back at GME's run from its bottom in mid-2020 to its full-blown frenzy, we'll see a remarkably consistent pattern in meme stocks this year. We've been tracking this trend closely due to its correlation with the five-year cycle. Below is GME in 2020 and KSS today, with the green arrow highlighting the last low in October before the parabolic rise. DORK stocks were mostly forgotten after the initial hype a week later, but they have been quietly and steadily rising since then. Stocks like BYND are starting to emerge, which is why I received the above notification from Yahoo Finance the other day. And if that wasn't enough to indicate improving market sentiment, Roundhill just this month relaunched their Meme Stock ETF, which they previously closed shortly after launching in late 2021 due to hitting highs. If the timing is accurate, I believe they're getting in early. Regarding the current macro landscape, my final point is IWM. As we all know, IWM (the Russell 2000 exchange-traded fund) is a signal of rising speculative activity. Its last clear breakout on the monthly chart was five years ago. We haven't seen a clear breakout and close yet, but if timing is any guide, we should see a strong close in November.

In Summary

Major market turning points are always difficult to grasp. We've been predicting every turning point in this cycle, and they always push you into a corner. The market always eliminates those with weak will and lack of confidence before the biggest moves.

The current market is no exception.

However, over time, we can see what the market is truly trying to tell us.

Given Bitcoin's daily, weekly, and monthly cycles, this volatility seems to mirror the shakeouts that preceded previous major moves. In November 2023, we saw news about ETFs. In November 2024, Trump, the "crypto president," was elected. What news will come in November 2025? I can't say for sure, but you can bet it'll arrive in about 10 days. If we focus solely on Bitcoin, a sharp rally in the short term is highly likely, but when we broaden our perspective and consider the context of stocks, gold, and risk-on behavior, I don't see how this is any different from what happened in late 2020. All of this is perfectly timed, as the market has been stuck in a stalemate for nearly a year, with participants giving up. However, if they study history, if they examine the timing, they'll see that this time is no different.

data.title: Tucker Carlson Gives A hard "NO" on Bitcoin, claiming it was created by the CIA and calling himself “a gold person”
data.description:
Tucker Carlson Suggests Bitcoin Creator Satoshi Nakamoto May Have CIA Ties

American television host and political commentator Tucker Carlson has stirred an uproar on the internet after suggesting that Satoshi is someone linked to the U.S intelligence agencies.

Carlson also expressed his doubts towards Bitcoin, calling it a scam run by the aristocrats of society.

"I fear that it will become so many other things in our country-a scam of sorts run by a coalition of the financial beneficiaries, like a few people who are getting all the winnings and the politicians they control, who use it to further their control of American society."

NEW: Tucker Carlson says he’s a hard "NO" on Bitcoin, claiming it was created by the CIA and calling himself “a gold person.”

“I fear it’ll become a scam run by financial elites and politicians to tighten control over society… Nobody can tell me who Satoshi is. I grew up in a… pic.twitter.com/epmMvdg6oz

— Bitcoin News (@BitcoinNewsCom) October 22, 2025

Speaking at a Turning Point USA event honoring the late activist Charlie Kirk, Carlson questioned Bitcoin’s legitimacy, claiming it may not be as decentralized as its supporters believe.

Identifying himself as “a gold buyer,” he said Bitcoin always lacked credibility to him because of the anomynous nature of its founder.

“Nobody can explain to me who Satoshi was — this mysterious guy who apparently died, but nobody knows who he was.”

He then went on to speculate how Satoshi could be someone from the establishment who is trying to secretly exert control over Americans

“I grew up in D.C., in a government family, so CIA, that’s my guess. Can’t prove it. But you’re asking me to invest in something whose founder is mysterious and holds billions in unused Bitcoin — what is that?”

Carlson’s remarks frame Bitcoin not as a technological breakthrough, but as a potential Trojan horse — a system that could be controlled by the very establishment it claims to challenge.

Satoshi Nakamoto, whose 2008 white paper introduced the concept of decentralized digital money, mined Bitcoin’s first block, the “Genesis Block,” in January 2009 before vanishing. More than fifteen years later, Nakamoto’s identity remains one of the most enduring mysteries in modern finance and technology.

Fact: Satoshi Nakamoto’s true identity remains unknown, with many theories but no proof — the name is widely believed to be a pseudonym.The true identity of Satoshi Nakamoto, the creator of Bitcoin, remains one of the greatest mysteries of the digital age.$Bitty is #Bitcoinpic.twitter.com/hsKSXkAFJD

— Sara $Bitty 🟠 (@Saracheginiasl) October 10, 2025

According to blockchain analytics firm Arkham Intelligence, Bitcoin addresses attributed to Satoshi hold roughly 1.096 million BTC, worth around $120 billion at current prices. Carlson’s comments arrive as Bitcoin trades near $108,800, steady after several weeks of consolidation — even as institutional adoption grows through U.S.-approved ETFs and major financial firms.

Bitcoiners Push Back Against the CIA Theory

Bitcoin advocates swiftly pushed back on Carlson’s claims. Marty Bent, founder of crypto media outlet TFTC, dismissed the notion that Bitcoin’s origins determine its trustworthiness.

“It doesn’t matter if the CIA created Bitcoin. Even if it did (it didn’t), anyone can audit the code to see if it works as intended. That’s all that matters.”

.@TuckerCarlson will come around when he realizes that it doesn't matter if the CIA created bitcoin.

Even if the CIA did (it didn't), anyone who is so driven and capable can audit the code to determine whether it does what it's supposed to do. That's all that matters. https://t.co/2VsBTmVsoW

— Marty Bent (@MartyBent) October 23, 2025

Jack Mallers, CEO of Strike, echoed that sentiment, arguing that Carlson “simply doesn’t understand Bitcoin.” He emphasized that Bitcoin’s transparency and open-source nature make it immune to centralized control — including from any government agency.

If you think knowing who created Bitcoin matters, you don’t understand it.

Bitcoin is open source. Nobody has special rights, and everyone can verify that. It doesn’t matter who created Bitcoin. Bitcoin is neutral technology, like math.

Who invented math, @TuckerCarlson? https://t.co/SZKzr5F2n8

— Jack Mallers (@jackmallers) October 23, 2025

For the Bitcoin community, Carlson’s theory reflects a fundamental misunderstanding: Bitcoin was designed to eliminate the need for trust in any individual or institution — including the CIA.

An Old Fear, A New Face

Speculation about Nakamoto’s identity has long captivated both crypto enthusiasts and skeptics. In June, an anonymous wallet sent roughly $20,000 worth of Bitcoin to Nakamoto’s Genesis Block address — the largest transaction to that wallet in months. Analysts at Arkham Intelligence suggested the transfer was either an exchange error or a symbolic tribute from an early Bitcoin supporter.

1 Bitcoin = $1,500,000

That’s the absolute base case pic.twitter.com/OUbiBBl93r

— Quinten | 048.eth (@QuintenFrancois) October 24, 2025

Such periodic transactions often reignite public fascination with Nakamoto’s identity. Yet despite countless theories — ranging from rogue developers to government agencies — no credible evidence has ever emerged linking Satoshi to the CIA or any intelligence organization.

Still, Carlson’s remarks underscore a persistent tension in the digital age: the fear that even technologies built for freedom might secretly serve the systems they sought to escape.

data.title: US National Debt Exceeds $38 Trillion: Will the Higher the Debt, the Higher the BTC Price?
data.description:
By Anthony Pompliano, Founder and CEO of Professional Capital Management

The United States of America is a first in many things. We were the first to write and ratify the Constitution. We were the first to send a man into space. We were the first to land on the moon. We were the first to build a commercial nuclear power plant. We were the first to ratify important human rights amendments like free speech and due process of law.

This week, the United States added another first. The U.S. national debt just surpassed $38 trillion, making us the first country in human history to accumulate such a massive debt.

Adam Kobeissi writes: "For the first time in history, the total U.S. debt has officially surpassed $38 trillion. This means that the total U.S. debt will increase by $500 billion this month, or $23 billion a day."

Give yourselves a round of applause.

Oh wait, this isn't a milestone we should celebrate. In fact, we should be shocked by the lack of fiscal discipline our country's leadership has to avoid this.

A big driver of our catastrophic fate is our addiction to printing money. Jesse Myers writes, "The printing press has never been hotter than it was before the COVID-19 pandemic. The global M2 money supply is now about $137 trillion. Just six months ago, it was $129 trillion." Lawrence Le Pard notes, "Global M2 is growing at an annualized rate of 12%. That's a far cry from the Fed's 2% target, and they haven't even really started the printing press yet." I don't think the national debt problem will go away in my lifetime. This means the currency will be devalued to avoid default, so politicians of both parties are effectively handing the debt to our children and grandchildren. This is awful and hopeless. The only thing I can do is take some of the economic value out of this broken system.

The higher the national debt, the higher Bitcoin will rise.

And it doesn’t look like they’re going to stop anytime soon.

data.title: Multicoin: Attention Assets and Prediction Markets
data.description:
Author: Eli Qian, Venture Partner at Multicoin Capital; Translator: Jinse Caijing xiaozou

For simplicity, we can broadly categorize assets into two types:

1. Cash Flow Assets—primarily stocks and bonds. These assets generate cash flows that investors value;

2. Supply and Demand Assets—primarily commodities and foreign exchange. Their prices fluctuate with supply and demand.

In recent years, the crypto space has spawned a new type of asset—assets whose value is measured in attention. Currently, "attention assets" primarily manifest as user-generated assets, such as NFTs, creator tokens, and memecoins. These assets serve as Schelling points for the tides of cultural attention, reflecting the ebb and flow of attention through price fluctuations. While Memecoin is culturally interesting, its financial attributes remain limited. An effective attention asset should allow market participants to gain exposure to the direct attention paid to a specific event. Through this mechanism, participants will be willing to trade assets they believe are mispriced, allowing the market to collectively form a price that reflects expectations of attention. We believe that, through appropriate structural design, attention assets have the potential to become a formal asset class. To advance this vision, this paper proposes the concept of an "attention oracle"—a novel oracle architecture that supports the creation of "attention perpetual contracts," allowing traders to trade long and short on the attention paid to cultural symbols. In short, an attention oracle collects binary prediction market data on a specific topic and, combining price, liquidity, and time dimensions, constructs a weighted composite index to capture fluctuations in attention. To ensure effective operation, the underlying market must be carefully selected to represent real-world attention input. Using prediction markets as a data source inherently imposes a cost for manipulation—malicious traders must invest capital to influence the index, which theoretically discourages tampering. 1. Why is there a need for perpetual attention contracts? User-generated assets (UGAs) have achieved product-market fit in the purely speculative sector and excel at tracking the attention of things that have been created from scratch, such as emerging online trends and memes. The core value of UGAs lies in creating assets for entities that cannot be accessed through traditional financial channels. Traditional asset issuance processes are slow, costly, and involve high regulatory barriers, significantly limiting the scope of assets. Attention assets, on the other hand, must maintain the speed of the internet to keep pace with the evolution of global thought. The combination of permissionless token issuance, smart pricing mechanisms like bonding curves, and decentralized exchanges enables anyone to create assets for free, guide liquidity, and open up trading globally. Observations show that UGAs typically start at zero. This isn't a flaw, but a feature—when you create a new meme, its initial attention starts at zero. Entering the market at a low point makes intuitive sense and allows early trend spotters to cash in on low-cost underlying assets. However, this also makes it difficult for UGAs to effectively track existing items with high attention. For example, suppose you're bullish on LeBron James's popularity and want to go long. While it's possible to create a memecoin, there are already dozens of LeBron tokens. How do you choose? Furthermore, a new coin must be created from scratch. As a top global celebrity, his attention should already be high; it's unlikely to skyrocket a hundredfold in a short period of time. What if you want to short his attention? Memecoins make this even more difficult. So, what characteristics should an asset with already high attention possess? The following requirements must be met:

  • It must have a two-way trading mechanism supporting long and short trades;

  • It must be anchored to a real-world attention benchmark;

  • The initial valuation should not be zero.

Taking a step back and examining these requirements, perpetual swaps perfectly meet the requirements: they offer two-way trading, an oracle pricing mechanism, and as derivatives, they don't need to be built from scratch. The real challenge lies in building an oracle system for perpetual attention swaps.

Some teams are already working on this problem, such as Noise. On this platform, traders can trade long and short on the community mindshare of crypto projects like MegaETH and Monad. Noise uses Kaito as its oracle, aggregating social media and news data to generate a numerical value representing topic popularity. However, the existing design still has room for improvement. The core goal of an attention oracle is to collect attention-related data and, through algorithmic processing, output a value indicator that can be used for long and short trading. The drawback of using social media as a data source is that it is easily manipulated—this confirms Goodhart’s Law: in adversarial markets, traders will attempt to manipulate pricing inputs. Kaito has already had to redesign its leaderboards and anti-spam filters to address this issue. Furthermore, social media is not a perfect measure of attention. Take Shohei Ohtani, for example: he has a global fan base across various social apps, data that Kaito may not fully capture. If he wins the World Series again, his popularity will increase further, but the number of followers and mentions may not increase linearly. 2. Attention Oracle: A Market-Based Solution Returning to the LeBron James example, suppose you want to trade his attention. The first step in building a LeBron attention oracle is to collect (or create if none already exist) multiple binary prediction markets about him, such as "Will LeBron James reach X million followers by the end of this month?", "Will LeBron win the championship in 2026?", and "Will LeBron be named MVP in 2026?" A complete oracle would require more underlying markets, but this example uses these three. The index price is calculated by weightedly aggregating the prices, liquidity, settlement time, and event importance of each market. For each prediction market, we consider the following four dimensions: price, liquidity, remaining settlement time, and event importance. To simplify the explanation, we use a basic weighting formula: each market's importance coefficient is scored on a scale of 1-10, and the weight is calculated based on liquidity and time factors. Assuming that the importance scores of the three markets are 8, 2 and 10 respectively, the weight of each market is calculated as follows: The final attention index is as follows: If we assume that the settlement periods of the three prediction markets are 180 days, 20 days, and 180 days respectively, and their event importance coefficients are 8, 2, and 10, respectively, the comprehensive calculation is as follows: Obviously, more complex calculations for the attention index exist, such as using open interest as a proxy for trading volume, accounting for correlated events, adjusting for market depth, and nonlinear relationships between variables. We've created an interactive website that allows readers to construct custom indices using the live Kalshi market. The primary advantage of this prediction market-based oracle approach is that manipulation incurs real costs. If a trader goes long on LeBron's attention and attempts to drive up the index, they must buy a position in the underlying binary prediction market. Assuming the underlying market is liquid, this means entering the position at a price that the market deems high. Another advantage, which will become increasingly important as the market expands, is that binary prediction markets provide market makers with a spot hedging channel. If a market maker goes short on the attention index, they can hedge their risk by taking a long position in the underlying prediction market that comprises the index. Adjacent has leveraged real-time, liquid markets on Kalshi to create political trend indices (e.g., Democrat vs. Republican, New York City mayoral election, etc.). We believe this approach can be generalized to track attention for any topic. As prediction markets develop, the range of feasible topics will continue to expand. 3. Design Trade-offs of Attention Oracles Our oracle architecture requires balancing multiple factors. When examining attention oracles from a broader perspective, the following are key considerations: the strength of the correlation between the input data; the practical feasibility of data acquisition; the level of controllability of the input variables; and the design of the algorithmic function used to calculate the attention index. The most significant trade-off with our proposed oracle solution is data accessibility. To build a LeBron James attention oracle, we first need to create multiple highly liquid prediction markets for related topics. These markets must maintain liquidity and be promptly replaced when old topics become ineffective. Therefore, this design is only suitable for niche, high-profile topics (such as Trump or Taylor Swift) for which established prediction markets already exist. Another paradox is that attention can increase regardless of the outcome of an event. For example, even if LeBron doesn't win another championship, discussions about his decline could actually drive up attention. In the real world, attention often flows to unexpected events, while prediction markets only measure the probability of an event occurring. If the market expects LeBron to be elected MVP but he doesn't, public discussion may intensify as the index drops, leading fans to argue that the selection was unfair. The optimal solution may be a hybrid approach that combines prediction markets, social media, and other data sources. Google Trends recently opened its search trend API to developers. Search volume and attention are clearly correlated, and its deduplication mechanism makes it more resistant to manipulation than social media metrics. LLMs can also be used to analyze easily manipulated data sources (such as mainstream media headlines or trending posts on Platform X) and filter out spam, thereby building a more robust evaluation system. We believe that established exchanges like Kalshi and Polymarket are best positioned to launch perpetual attention contracts, as they already have a large, liquid underlying market and a user base. However, the opportunities in attention assets are not limited to industry giants. One possible solution is to establish a dedicated vault for trading prediction markets, specifically long or shorting specific themes. For example, a "Long Taylor Swift Vault" could buy "yes" contracts for events like her songs reaching the top ten or her Super Bowl performance. Vault managers would then determine which markets correlate with increased attention. Another model utilizes Hyperliquid's builders to deploy perpetual contract functionality. The HIP-3 proposal gives market implementers the flexibility to define their own oracles—indices can be constructed by combining data sources such as Kalshi/Polymarket prices, social media metrics, Google search trends, and news headlines. 4. The Potential of Attention Assets Ironically, the first mature application scenario of the attention economy may appear in the stock market. Stock prices are composed of two major components: the discounted cash flow value (i.e., intrinsic value) and meme value. Historically, most stocks have not possessed significant meme value. However, in recent years, with the rise of Wall Street gambling forums and 24/5 retail trading platforms like Robinhood, more and more stocks have begun to carry meme value. The core task of stock research analysts is to determine stock prices. While there are established methods for calculating DCF components, how can one quantify the value of memes? As more assets are traded based on meme value, developing meme value modeling methods is imperative. Professional investors have begun using metrics like follower count, likes, and exposure to assess market sentiment, and prediction markets and other oracle constructions can become effective tools for measuring stock attention and optimizing trading models.

However, the potential of attention assets goes far beyond stock pricing. We believe that predicting attention is an economically valuable activity—attention is a leading indicator of consumer preferences and spending. Companies allocate R&D, recruitment, and marketing budgets based on the flow of attention. The key lies in developing new heuristic models to track this flow.

data.title: The Nature of Polymarket's Financial Derivatives and the Arbitrage Logic of Professional Traders
data.description:
Author: Danny; Source: X, @agintender The characterization of prediction markets has sparked heated debate over their financial nature and gambling nature. This article attempts to analyze how Polymarket, through its unique architecture and mechanisms, integrates its event contracts into a new type of binary options financial derivative on the blockchain, avoiding gambling elements. Unlike the traditional bookmaker betting model, Polymarket uses a P2P CLOB for matching and settlement, eliminating the traditional gambling element of "house advantage." This model and architecture not only eliminates the "betting element," attracting professional market makers to provide deep liquidity, but also creates vast opportunities for maneuvering, including market rebalancing arbitrage and combination arbitrage. Where there is gambling, there are disagreements — where there are disagreements, there are interests — and where there are interests, there is a market. Beyond a gambling platform, Polymarket has become a "decentralized magic crystal ball" driven by professional capital, aggregating collective wisdom and pricing risk. 1. Why Not Gambling? Institutional Nature: P2P Trading and the Lack of "House Advantage" 1.1 Event-Based Binary Options Contracts: Conceptual Analysis Polymarket's core trading product is called an event contract, whose structure is similar to the binary options model in traditional financial markets. The price of a share for each event outcome fluctuates between 0.00 and 1.00, with a constant total of 1.00. The share price directly reflects the market's real-time probability of the event occurring.

Definition and Structure of Binary Options

A binary option is a special type of derivative that derives its name from its fixed, binary payout structure: at contract expiration, either a predetermined fixed payout is received ("in-the-money") or nothing is received ("out-of-the-money"). This type of contract only involves directional predictions about events, without involving leverage, margin, or complex settlement mechanisms, making it a simplified form of futures.

Polymarket Event Contract Integration

Polymarket's event contracts are a digital, decentralized implementation of this model:

  1. Fixed Payout: When an event is finalized, shares representing the correct outcome are settled at a fixed value of $1.00 USDC; shares representing incorrect outcomes are settled at $0.00 USDC.

  2. Probabilistic Pricing: The price of event shares purchased by users fluctuates between $0.00 and $1.00. This price reflects the market's collective assessment of the probability of the event occurring. For example, if YES shares are trading at 0.36, it means the market believes the probability of the event occurring is 36%.

  3. Fully Collateralized Constraint: The price of YES shares plus the price of NO shares in a market "should" always equal $1.00 USDC, ensuring the market's zero-sum nature and full collateralization.

  4. Probabilistic Pricing: The price of event shares purchased by users fluctuates between $0.00 and $1.00. This price reflects the market's collective assessment of the probability of the event occurring. For example, if YES shares are trading at 0.36, it means the market believes the probability of the event occurring is 36%.

  5. Fully Collateralized Constraint: The price of YES shares plus the price of NO shares in a market "should" always equal $1.00 USDC, ensuring the market's zero-sum nature and full collateralization.

  6. Judgment method: Use blockchain oracles to determine YES/NO results.

Therefore, Polymarket's event contracts are essentially blockchain-based, fully collateralized binary options, without dealer betting. They transform collective judgments on future events into tradable financial assets.

1.2 Difference from Traditional Gambling Model

Traditional bookmakers (such as sportsbooks) act as the "dealer" or counterparty, actively betting against gamblers and extracting a high house advantage (House Edge/VIG) to ensure long-term profits. Polymarket, on the other hand, is a pure P2P exchange, providing only matching and settlement services (assuming Polymarket does not provide a handicap). The platform completes trades by matching two traders with opposing views, without taking any risk on market outcomes. Its revenue comes from collecting trading commissions from the winners of successfully settled contracts.

2. Order Book Architecture: Professional Market Making and Liquidity Incentives

Polymarket abandoned its earlier automated market maker (AMM/LMSR) model and turned to a limit order book (CLOB).

2.1 Professional Operational Space Provided by CLOB

The CLOB architecture allows traders to set bids and asks through limit orders. This seemingly complex operation creates operational space and profit opportunities for professional market makers:

  • Earning the Bid-Ask Spread: Market makers provide deep liquidity to the market by offering two-sided quotes between the bid and ask prices, thereby earning the small difference between the bid and ask prices.

  • Position and Risk Management: Market makers' core responsibilities include position management. They sell at high prices (sell) and buy at low prices (buy) based on their own probability models, managing market exposure by dynamically adjusting orders and continuously pushing prices toward what they believe to be the "true probability" level.

  • Liquidity Rewards: To further incentivize market makers, Polymarket has implemented a liquidity reward mechanism that pays daily rewards based on the competitiveness (how close the price is to the market midpoint) and size of the order.

2.2 Hybrid Architecture and On-Chain Settlement

3. Professional Arbitrage Strategies: The "Invisible Hand" of Market Efficiency

3.1 Intra-Market Arbitrage

This is the most basic form of arbitrage, which ensures that the market price satisfies the constraint of YES share price + NO share price = 1.00.

When the market price deviates, for example, the YES share price is When the price of the NO share is 0.58 and the price of the NO share is 0.40, for a total of 0.98, the arbitrageur will immediately buy both shares at a total cost of 0.98 and lock in a return of 1.00 at contract settlement, thereby earning a risk-free profit of 0.02.

This arbitrage activity is the market's self-correcting algorithm, forcing the price to quickly return to 1.00.

3.2 Combinatorial Arbitrage and Multiple Logical Chains

This type of arbitrage exploits the logical or probabilistic associations between different events, such as the multi-chain logic between the "probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates" and the "value of the US dollar," or the association between the "probability of a sharpshooter winning MVP" and the "probability of the MVP's team winning the championship."

When pricing in multiple interconnected markets is inconsistent, arbitrageurs will eliminate this mispricing through cross-market hedging and trading, thereby locking in combined profits.

3.3 Cross-Platform Hedging and Regulatory Arbitrage

Professional traders do use Polymarket as a hedging tool to hedge risks with traditional financial markets or other platforms. For example:

Traders can short the outcome of a political event on Polymarket (for example, by purchasing NO shares) while establishing a hedge position in the traditional futures or options market to protect their overall portfolio from the impact of the event. .

Take advantage of the slight price differences that may occur between Polymarket and other regulated competitors (such as Kalshi) or traditional gambling exchanges (such as Bet365) to conduct cross-platform buying and selling to lock in risk-free profits.

4. Conclusion

Polymarket's CLOB structure and P2P trading mechanism make it highly similar in function to traditional financial derivatives markets. It transforms collective beliefs about uncertain future events into tradable assets by providing a zero-sum, no-bank advantage trading environment.

Platform efficiency does not come from algorithms, but from the market game brought about by arbitrage—enforced by professional market makers and arbitrageurs through precise position management and multi-dimensional arbitrage strategies. Therefore, Polymarket It has transcended the realm of "online gambling" to become a highly efficient, arbitrage-driven, decentralized financial infrastructure for information aggregation and risk pricing. Will the truth become clearer through debate? We don't know. Can the market predict the future through full market competition? Let's wait and see. Beyond a gambling platform, Polymarket has become a "new-century magic crystal ball" driven by professional capital, used to aggregate collective wisdom and price risk.

data.title: From fragmented regulation to systematic governance: interpreting the EU MiCAR regulatory framework
data.description:
1. Introduction: From Regulatory Gaps to a Unified Framework In 2023, the European Union officially launched the landmark Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCAR). Against the backdrop of the gradual maturity of global crypto-asset regulation, the EU introduced the MiCAR regulatory framework, aiming to establish a unified crypto-asset regulatory framework across the 27 EU member states, replacing the previously fragmented regulatory practices across each member state.

According to the EU legislative process, MiCAR will be implemented in stages:

·From June 30, 2024, the key provisions related to stablecoins (ART and EMT parts) will be officially applicable;

·From December 30, 2024, the remaining provisions on crypto asset service provider (CASP) licensing, market manipulation prevention, investor protection, etc. will take full effect.

The background of MiCAR can be traced back to the "Digital Finance Strategy" proposed by the European Union in 2020. The core goal of this strategy is to balance innovation and regulation, protect investors and maintain financial stability. Under this framework, MiCAR, together with regulations such as the Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA), constitute the core regulatory system for digital finance in the EU. More importantly, MiCAR is not just a "risk prevention" regulation, but the EU hopes to provide legal certainty for the long-term sustainable development of the blockchain and crypto industries through technology-neutral legislation, making it more practical. Below, this article will interpret the main contents of the MiCAR framework, such as the definition of crypto assets and asset reference tokens, and analyze the impact of this framework on the European crypto market.

2. Main contents of the MiCAR regulatory framework

MiCAR's regulatory system can be divided into two levels: crypto-assets and crypto-asset service providers.

2.1 Definition and classification of crypto-assets

On a technology-neutral basis, MiCAR defines "crypto-asset" as "a digital representation of value or rights that can be transferred and stored electronically through distributed ledger technology (DLT) or similar technologies." It divides crypto assets into three core categories, as follows: 2.1.1 Asset-Referenced Token (ART) ART is a cryptocurrency, distinct from an electronic money token (EMT), whose value is stabilized by reference to some other value, equity, or a combination thereof (Article 3, paragraph 1, item 6 of the MiCAR). Pursuant to Articles 16 and 20 of the MiCAR, the entity proposing to issue an ART must complete an authorization procedure prior to issuance, and the issuer must be a legal person or authorized entity established in the EU. The authorization procedure must be initiated through a formal application (Article 18 of the MiCAR). In addition, the application must include a legal opinion confirming that the cryptocurrency exists, falls within the scope of the MiCAR definition, and is not an electronic money token (EMT). Finally, the proposed issuer must submit a white paper for the cryptocurrency, which can only be issued after approval. 2.1.2 Electronic Money Token (EMT, similar to a stablecoin) The value of an electronic money token is intended to be maintained by pegging it to the value of an official currency. These tokens can be considered stablecoins pegged to a single official currency (such as the euro or the US dollar). They are specifically defined in MiCAR and subject to specific regulation. According to Article 81, paragraph 1 of MiCAR, only credit institutions or electronic money institutions may issue electronic money tokens (EMTs). Furthermore, since EMTs are legally classified as electronic money, issuers must comply with Chapters II and III of the Electronic Money Directive (EMD). MiCAR does not prescribe an authorization procedure for EMT issuers; they only need to notify the authorities and publish a white paper. 2.1.3 Other Cryptocurrencies These cryptocurrencies, such as utility tokens and Bitcoin, are neither asset reference tokens (ARTs) nor electronic money tokens (EMTs), and are not excluded from MiCAR. Therefore, they generally do not require an issuance license. In principle, these cryptocurrencies still require the preparation of a white paper, notification to the authorities, and public publication, but exemptions exist if certain conditions are met. 2.2 Crypto-Asset Service Provider (CASP) System MiCAR has established for the first time a unified regulatory system for crypto-asset service providers, proposing systematic regulatory requirements for crypto-asset service providers (CASPs), covering services such as custody, trading, exchange, consulting, issuance, and transfer. The core requirements for CASPs include: 2.2.1 Unified Licensing System (passporting): Once a CASP obtains a MiCAR license in any member state, it can operate throughout the EU; this is known as the EU passporting mechanism. The core of MiCAR is to bring all companies providing cryptoasset services to EU users under the unified regulatory framework of CASPs. Any CASP wishing to operate within the EU must obtain authorization in any member state. They can then serve the entire EU market through the "single license" principle. Furthermore, MiCAR specifies 10 categories of service activities. Any company engaging in any of these activities within the EU must obtain a MiCAR license and be subject to regulatory constraints.

This classification system covers almost all major business forms in today's crypto market. This also means that whether it is a mature large-scale trading platform or an innovative project in the early stages, as long as it provides relevant services to EU users, it must be included in the MiCAR supervision scope. 2.2.2 Transitional Arrangements: To ensure a smooth transition, MiCAR establishes transitional provisions: CASPs that have been operating in compliance with national laws before December 30, 2024, will be allowed to continue operating during the transition period until they obtain or are denied a MiCAR license, or cease operations on July 1, 2026 at the latest. Member States may set their own transition periods, the length of which varies from country to country. This arrangement provides the market with an 18-month buffer period, allowing regulators and industry players ample time to conduct institutional integration and compliance adjustments. It also effectively resolves the previous problem of "multiple regulation" within the EU, making the regulatory environment more certain and competitively fair.

3. Impact on the crypto tax regulatory landscape

The introduction of MiCAR is not only an update to the regulatory system, but also has a profound impact on the EU's tax policy and compliance regulatory landscape. 3.1 Issuance Supervision: From White Paper Disclosure to Reserve Constraints 3.1.1 Ordinary Cryptoasset Issuance: White Paper Disclosure + Light Regulation Under the MiCAR system, for ordinary cryptoassets that do not fall under ART or EMT, supervision adopts a milder approach of "disclosure first, approval second." First, the issuer must be a company or legal entity with legal entity status so that its actions can be legally traceable and accountable, and even in the event of disputes, accountability can be pursued. Secondly, the issuer must draft and publish a crypto-asset white paper in accordance with MiCAR requirements, disclosing key information, including but not limited to: the issuer's name, registered address, and governance structure; the technical architecture, working principles, and equity mechanism of the issued token; risk disclosures (such as smart contract risks, liquidity risks, and policy risks); investor rights and obligations, fee structure, and issuance/burning mechanisms; and compliance statements (e.g., phrases such as "This white paper has not been approved by EU competent authorities" to avoid misleading investors into believing it is officially endorsed). Furthermore, MiCAR requires issuers to implement an ongoing update obligation regarding significant changes. This means that if changes occur in project structure, funding arrangements, risk factors, and other factors that may affect investment decisions, the white paper should be revised promptly or a revised explanation should be disclosed to ensure investors always have the latest and most accurate information.

Under this mechanism, projects do not need to go through complicated pre-approval procedures, thereby lowering the entry threshold and facilitating the participation of innovators and small projects in the market. At the same time, through the design of information disclosure and accountability mechanisms, it can also balance the protection of investors' right to know and the maintenance of market vitality.

3.1.2 Stablecoins: Strong Regulation + Rigid Constraints on Reserves

Unlike the relatively relaxed issuance system mentioned above, MiCAR imposes a strict and rigid regulatory framework on the issuance of stablecoins, namely ART and EMT, to ensure the robustness of these tokens in terms of redemption, reserves, and security.

（1） Authorization requirements and white paper approval

From June 30, 2024, all projects that publicly issue ART or EMT in the EU, or list on exchanges, must be authorized by the competent authorities of the country where they are located.

In the case of ART, issuers other than credit institutions must apply for MiCAR authorization and must submit a white paper during the authorization process, which can only be issued after review by the competent authorities.

For EMT, the issuer must be a credit institution or an Electronic Money Institution (EMI), authorized in accordance with the traditional Electronic Money Directive (EMD) or other regulatory frameworks.

After the white paper is submitted, the competent authority must determine its completeness and compliance with regulatory requirements within a specified time period; if it does, it will approve or file it.

MiCAR also recognizes that some ARTs or EMTs may be large due to their size and other factors, and thus may pose higher risks. Therefore, the European Banking Authority (EBA) will assume supervisory responsibility for the issuance functions of institutions that issue important ARTs and some important EMTs under MiCAR.

（2） Segregation of Reserves and Assets

The segregation of reserves and assets is the most critical part of MiCAR’s regulatory design: the issuer must establish a pool of reserve assets that is isolated from its other assets and is used first to guarantee the redemption requests of token holders. In other words, even if the issuer goes bankrupt, this part of the reserve assets should not be used to repay debts or liquidate to other creditors.

The requirements for the composition and liquidity of reserves are also very strict:

· Reserves must be diversified and can only include highly liquid, low-risk assets (such as deposits, government bonds, high-quality covered bonds, certain money market instruments, etc.). Regarding the deposit ratio with credit institutions, the EBA's draft Regulatory Technical Standard (RTS) published in 2024 recommends that at least 30% of the funds of non-significant stablecoins be deposited in banks to ensure basic redemption capabilities. If the stablecoin is deemed significant, a 60% deposit is required. Furthermore, the issuer should be able to promptly liquidate reserve assets when token holders request redemption. (Please refer to the original text of RTS: Article 36(1) of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 requires issuers of asset-referenced tokens (ARTs), whether they are either if the ARTs are significant ARTs or not, to constitute and maintain a reserve of assets at all times to cover their liabilities against the holders of their issued ARTs matching the risks reflected within these liabilities. The reserve of assets is composed of the assets received when issuing the token holders and by the highly liquid financial instruments the issuer may invest in. In the case of tokens referenced to official currencies, a minimum part of the reserves should be held in the form of deposits in credit institutions (at least 30% of the amount referenced in each official currency if the token is not significant, and at least 60% if the token is significant). Upon redemption requests from token holders, the issuers should be able to liquidate the reserve assets.)

4. Recommendations for Investors and Institutions

Faced with the systemic regulatory shift brought about by MiCAR, European investors and crypto companies should adopt proactive compliance and risk management strategies.

4.1 Investor Level: Strengthening Tax Compliance and Filing

Systemic regulatory shifts have created a need for automated tax compliance tools. For institutional investors with larger business volumes and more complex structures, relying solely on personalized tools is insufficient to meet compliance and audit requirements. Individual investors can also use such tools to record transaction and income data in real time, enabling automated tax filing and reporting, improving efficiency and accuracy. Take FinTax Suite, for example. This modular system seamlessly integrates with mainstream ERP systems. Through its intelligent rules engine and multi-dimensional reporting system, it covers key aspects such as data capture, automated accounting, report generation, and compliance audits, helping companies achieve financial transparency and tax compliance within a global regulatory environment. FinTax Suite also supports audit-ready GAAP/IFRS-compliant financial reporting, a dual-account system for stablecoins and fiat currencies, AI-OCR invoice recognition, and bank statement import, providing a comprehensive financial and tax management solution for on-chain payment and high-frequency trading companies. Furthermore, multinational investors should pay attention to the cross-border reporting requirements under DAC8, which clarify the differences in capital gains tax and VAT among EU member states. 4.2 Institutional Level: Preparing for MiCAR License Applications in Advance For crypto exchanges, custodians, and wallet providers, obtaining MiCAR authorization is a prerequisite for entering the EU market. Institutions planning to enter the European market should communicate with EU member state regulators in advance to clarify the length of their respective transition periods. While MiCAR authorization may be a high barrier to entry, obtaining it grants access to the entire EU market, which can provide a significant competitive advantage in the long term. Third-country companies wishing to provide cryptoasset services in the EU must also establish an entity within the EU and apply for CASP authorization in accordance with MiCAR. The only exception is so-called "reverse solicitation," where clients initiate service requests entirely on their own initiative. It's important to note that the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA)'s final report on reverse solicitation aims to tighten the scope of reverse solicitation under the MiCAR framework. Non-EU platforms that reach EU clients through reverse solicitation without authorization may expose investors to legal risks. 5. Conclusion: MiCAR: Balancing Regulation and Innovation The introduction of the EU's MiCAR marks the official transition of crypto assets from their wild growth phase to a more mature and regulated mainstream financial system in Europe. It both addresses risks and provides an institutional foundation for innovation. In the coming years, the integration of MiCAR with regulations such as DAC8 and DORA will foster a more transparent, secure, and efficient crypto market. For investors, compliance is no longer a burden, but a safeguard for legitimate and long-term returns. For businesses, while MiCAR imposes a threshold, it also serves as a passport to entry into one of the world's largest crypto markets. For all market participants, the implementation of MiCAR is not only a comprehensive compliance test, but also a critical window to seize opportunities and achieve business growth. Only by proactively adapting to regulatory trends and deeply integrating compliance concepts into corporate strategy and operations can we remain invincible in the new competitive landscape.

data.title: Tiger Research Report: Bitcoin Valuation to $200,000 in Q4 2025
data.description:
Author: Daniel Kim, Ryan Yoon, Jay Jo; Source: Tiger Research; Compiler: Shaw Golden Finance

This report presents Tiger Research’s outlook for Bitcoin in the fourth quarter of 2025, with a target of $200,000, citing continued buying by institutional investors amid market volatility, the Federal Reserve’s rate cuts, and the October plunge that confirmed institutional dominance in the market.

Key Points

  • Institutional investors continued to increase their holdings amid volatility - ETF net inflows remained stable in the third quarter, and MSTR increased its holdings by 388 bitcoins in a single month, strengthening its belief in long-term investment;

  • Overheated but not yet extreme - The MVRV-Z index was 2.31, indicating that valuations were high but not yet extreme. The liquidation of leveraged funds cleared out short-term traders, creating room for the next wave of increases;

  • The global liquidity environment continued to improve - The broad money supply (M2) exceeded US$96 trillion, a record high. Expectations of a Fed rate cut have increased, and it is expected that there will be 1-2 more rate cuts this year. Institutional investors bought amidst Sino-US trade uncertainty. In the third quarter of 2025, the Bitcoin market slowed from its strong second-quarter gains (up 28% month-over-month) and entered a period of volatile sideways trading (up 1% month-over-month). On October 6, Bitcoin hit a record high of $126,210. However, the Trump administration's renewed trade pressure on China caused the price of Bitcoin to pull back 18% to $104,000, significantly increasing volatility. According to Volmex Finance's Bitcoin Volatility Index (BVIV), Bitcoin volatility narrowed from March to September as institutional investors steadily increased their holdings. However, it surged 41% after September, exacerbating market uncertainty (Chart 1). This pullback appears temporary, driven by renewed Sino-US trade friction and Trump's hawkish rhetoric. Institutional strategic buying, led by Strategy Inc. (MSTR), is actually accelerating. The macroeconomic environment also contributed to the market's upward trend. Global broad money supply (M2) exceeded $96 trillion, a record high, while the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points to a range of 4.00%-4.25% on September 17. The Fed hinted at one or two more rate cuts this year. A stable labor market and a recovering economy are creating favorable conditions for risky assets. Institutional inflows remained strong. Net inflows into Bitcoin spot ETFs reached $7.8 billion in the third quarter. While lower than the $12.4 billion in Q2, net inflows throughout Q3 confirm steady buying from institutional investors. This momentum continued into Q4, with $3.2 billion recorded in the first week of October alone, a record high for weekly inflows in 2025. This suggests that institutional investors view price corrections as strategic entry opportunities. Strategy continued buying during the market correction, purchasing 220 BTC on October 13 and 168 BTC on October 20, for a total of 388 BTC in one week. This demonstrates that institutional investors remain steadfast in their belief in Bitcoin's long-term value, regardless of short-term volatility. On-chain data signals overheating, but fundamentals remain unchanged. On-chain analysis reveals some signs of overheating, though valuations are not yet worrisome. The MVRV-Z indicator (market capitalization to realized value ratio) is currently in overheated territory at 2.31, but has stabilized relative to the extreme valuation range approached in July and August (Exhibit 2). The net unrealized profit and loss ratio (NUPL) also showed overheating territory, but has eased from the high unrealized profit situation in the second quarter (Figure 3). The adjusted expenditure-output profit ratio (aSOPR), which reflects investors' realized profits and losses, is very close to its equilibrium value of 1.03, suggesting no cause for concern (Chart 4). Bitcoin's transaction count and active user count remained at similar levels compared to the previous quarter, suggesting a temporary slowdown in network growth (Chart 5). Meanwhile, total transaction volume is trending upward. A decrease in the number of transactions coupled with an increase in volume suggests that larger amounts of funds are being moved in fewer transactions, suggesting an increase in large-scale capital flows. However, we cannot simply interpret the increase in trading volume as a positive sign. Recent increases in funds flowing into centralized exchanges generally indicate that holders are preparing to sell (Chart 6). In the absence of improvements in fundamental indicators such as transaction counts and active users, the increase in trading volume is more indicative of short-term capital flows and selling pressure amidst high volatility, rather than genuine demand. The October 11th Crash Demonstrates a Shift in Market Dominance from Institutional to Institutional. The October 11th crash on centralized exchanges (a 14% drop) demonstrates the Bitcoin market's shift from retail to institutional dominance. The key point is this: the market reaction is radically different from previous ones. In a similar environment at the end of 2021, panic spread among retail investors, leading to a subsequent crash. This time, the pullback was limited. Following a large-scale liquidation, institutional investors continued to buy, demonstrating their resolute defense of the market's downside potential. Furthermore, institutions appear to view this as a healthy consolidation that will help eliminate excessive speculative demand. In the short term, the series of sell-offs will lower the average purchase price for retail investors and increase psychological pressure, potentially exacerbating volatility due to dampened market sentiment. However, if institutional investors continue to enter the market during this period of sideways trading, this pullback could lay the foundation for the next leg of gains. Target Price Raised to $200,000 Using our TVM methodology for Q3 analysis, we arrive at a neutral base price of $154,000, a 14% increase from $135,000 in Q2. To this end, we apply a -2% fundamental adjustment and a +35% macro adjustment, arriving at a target price of $200,000. The -2% fundamental adjustment reflects a temporary slowdown in network activity and increased deposits on centralized exchanges, indicating short-term weakness. The macro adjustment remains at 35%. Continued global liquidity expansion and institutional inflows, along with the Federal Reserve's rate cuts, provide strong catalysts for a fourth-quarter rally. A short-term pullback may stem from signs of overheating, but this represents a healthy correction rather than a trend or shift in market perception. The continued rise in the base price indicates a steady increase in Bitcoin's intrinsic value. Despite the temporary weakness, the medium- to long-term upside outlook remains solid.

data.title: CZ Crushes Peter Schiff’s Dream of Tokenizing Gold on the Blockchain With One Tweet
data.description:
Peter Schiff Calls For A Return Back To Gold As He Launches Tokenized Gold

In one of the most ironic statements made this year, long-time Bitcoin critic Peter Schiff has just started a campaign to draw people to tokenise gold at a time when gold is experiencing a 25% trillion plunge in its value this week.

In his interview on the ThreadGuy podcast, Schiff announced his plans to launch his own tokenized gold product, where he claimed users would be able to buy and store gold in a vault via an app. Users would also be able to transfer ownership through a blockchain, or redeem it for physical gold.

Peter Schiff reveals he will be launching a tokenized gold product

"You'll be able to buy gold on an app through your phone, the gold will be stored in a vault and then you will be able to effortlessly transfer ownership of gold to people you know or redeem it for physical gold" pic.twitter.com/mWCXVKj9v8

— Tengen (@Crypto_Tengen) October 23, 2025

Schiff used the imminent fall of bitcoin for why people should be jumping on the gold bandwagon, stressing that Bitcoin has no intrinsic value and will eventually "go to zero." He also called Bitcoin a gigantic pump-and-dump, driven by early adopter cashing out at the expense of newer investors.

"I still think it's going to zero, what I am underestimating was the gullibility of the public and the marketing savvy of those promoting it."

Schiff also claims that the world is entering another "debt crisis", one that would surpass the financial crisis in 2008. He claimed that this debt crisis would not only cause hyperinflation, but it will also trigger the collapse of US treasury bonds and gold prices will soar beyond $4,000 per ounce. The dollar’s dominance, he said, is ending — with the world inevitably returning to a gold-based monetary system.

Gold’s Rough Week Adds to the Irony

Gold’s market performance, however, seems to undercut Schiff’s timing. The metal lost about 8% in just two days — a staggering $2.5 trillion wipeout in total market cap, according to The Kobeissi Letter. The sell-off followed a rapid rally earlier in the year that saw prices climb 60% amid inflation fears and global instability.

Gold was shining like never before… until it wasn’t. ✨

In its biggest one-day fall since 2013, spot gold just crashed 6.3%, wiping out weeks of euphoria in a single session.

Even the “safe haven” isn’t safe when greed peaks.
Markets have a brutal way of testing conviction,… pic.twitter.com/jNwvS2WZoY

— Ashish Kumar Meher (@AshishMeher7) October 21, 2025

Despite Schiff’s insistence that the drop is temporary, the moment underscores how volatile even “safe haven” assets can be. For many investors, that volatility only reinforces the case for Bitcoin — an asset with fixed supply, global liquidity, and no reliance on vaults or custodians.

Schiff's campaign has drawn skepticism, including CZ who calls tokenised gold a "trust me bro" asset that doesn’t represent true on-chain ownership but rather something that relies on a mutual promise.

“It’s tokenizing that you trust some third party will give you gold at some later date — even after their management changes, maybe decades later, during a war.”

Peter Schiff right now: Gold is gold bro, trust me! 😭💰 CZ just called it a ‘trust me bro token’ 💀

CZ just ended tokenized gold in one tweet 💣
Peter Schiff left the chat 😂 pic.twitter.com/gSNoVfLqns

— Anu (@anu_btc) October 24, 2025

CZ’s comments highlight a broader skepticism among crypto purists toward asset-backed tokens. Unlike decentralized cryptocurrencies, tokenized commodities like Schiff’s depend entirely on the credibility of their custodians — the exact kind of middleman crypto was designed to remove.

While Schiff insists his gold system offers transparency and utility, critics say it simply rebrands centralized trust in a digital wrapper. For CZ, the idea runs counter to everything blockchain stands for: autonomy, verifiability, and self-custody.

$trust me bro
0x44441412b008cb20f06685780fd5aaf916cba63d
currently 700 k Mc@cz_binance plans is debating @PeterSchiff and is saying that Peter Schiff's tokenized gold as custodial, not truly on-chain, needing trusted third parties aka
"Trust me bro". https://t.co/FygnopmyVWpic.twitter.com/JrgbtQ4Cf3

— cope (@OnChainCope) October 23, 2025

Many in the crypto community have also pointed out the hypocracy of how a self-proclaimed “enemy of Bitcoin” is now embracing blockchain technology to modernize the very asset he claims will replace crypto — and doing so at a time when gold has suffered its steepest two-day drop since 2013.

Gold May Glitter, But Bitcoin Still Shines Brighter

Schiff’s tokenized gold experiment is more than ironic — it’s emblematic of a broader shift. Even the most hardened gold advocates are now acknowledging that blockchain technology is here to stay. But while tokenization may modernize gold, it doesn’t change its core limitation: it still relies on trust, storage, and intermediaries.

Bitcoin, by contrast, doesn’t ask for permission — or forgiveness. It runs 24/7, audits itself every ten minutes, and exists beyond borders and politics. Gold may continue to shine in vaults, but Bitcoin continues to dominate in code. And for an asset born to replace “trust me bro” promises, that might be the ultimate proof of resilience.

data.title: Federal Reserve's "Lite Account": Promoting the Integration of AI and Crypto Payments
data.description:
Author: Zhang Feng

The historic closed-door meeting of the Federal Reserve quietly changed the position of cryptocurrencies in the global financial system. On October 21, at the inaugural "Payment Innovation Conference" held at the Federal Reserve headquarters, Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller explicitly stated that "the DeFi industry will no longer be viewed as suspicious or ridiculed, and the Federal Reserve will actively embrace payment innovation." This "historic" meeting was unusual in that it no longer viewed cryptocurrencies as a fringe threat to the financial system, but rather as a key component of the future payment system.

What is even more striking is that the Federal Reserve has proposed the specific concept of a "lite master account" to provide basic payment services to non-bank payment companies. This means that

stablecoin issuers and crypto payment companies are expected to directly access the Federal Reserve's payment system and break away from their dependence on traditional banks.

1. From suspicion to embrace, the Federal Reserve's attitude has changed dramatically

Global payment innovation has entered a period of acceleration, with cross-border applications of stablecoins, tokenization of traditional assets, and AI-driven payment security technologies becoming industry focus. Against this backdrop, the Federal Reserve chose not to resist but to actively participate and guide innovation. This meeting marked a fundamental shift in US regulatory attitudes toward crypto assets and AI-powered payments. The Fed's shift from past caution and skepticism to current openness and acceptance demonstrates its recognition of the trend toward financial innovation. In his speech, Governor Waller elaborated on the Fed's dual role in payment innovation: serving as a convener to resolve coordination issues and operating core payment and settlement infrastructure. He emphasized that the Fed is "looking ahead and conducting practical research on tokenization, smart contracts, and the intersection of AI and payments." This shift in attitude reflects the Fed's clear understanding of the current state of the payments revolution.

II. AI and the New Financial Era of Asset Tokenization
